language_description: Set your personal language and timezone. These override workspace defaults for your account only.
personal_override_hint: Your personal language and timezone can override the workspace defaults.
profile_language_save_failed: Failed to update language and timezone
password_description: Update your account password used for login.
password_min_length: New password must be at least 8 characters.
passwords_must_match: New password and confirm password must match.

ops.title: Jobs / Ops
ops.delete_terminal_only: Cancel queued or running jobs before deleting them.
ops.no_preview: No preview available for this job.
ops.delete_job_body: This will permanently remove this terminal job record and its events. This cannot be undone.

auth.password_reset_failed: Failed to send password reset email.
auth.signed_in_as: Signed in as {{email}}. Set your password to continue.
auth.open_from_email_link: Open this page from a valid invite or password reset email link.

audit.subtitle: System and module activity
audit.footer: Audit feed will populate as system activity grows.

data_explorer.install_app_to_see_data: Install/enable an app to see data.
data_explorer.choose_entity: Choose an entity from the left panel to view records.
data_explorer.entity_unavailable_description: This entity is not available or the module is disabled.
data_explorer.no_list_view_description: This entity has no list view in its manifest.

integrations.disable_before_delete: Disable selected connections before deleting them.
integrations.delete_body: This will permanently remove {{count}} disabled integration connection(s). This cannot be undone.
integrations.how_it_works_body: Choose the provider template first. After creation you will land on the connection page to fill setup fields, attach secrets, and run a connection test.
integrations.next_step_body: Configure setup, attach secrets, then test the connection.

secrets.title: Connection secrets
secrets.help: Link the named secret slots this provider expects. Secrets stay reusable and encrypted, and the worker resolves them only at runtime.
secrets.none_declared: This provider does not declare any named secrets.
secrets.not_linked: No secret linked yet. Add or select a {{slot}} secret for this connection.
secrets.matching_pairs_help: Matching provider and secret-key pairs are shown first so setup stays predictable.

request.title: Manual request
request.help: Use this to test the connection against a real endpoint. This is especially useful while setting up Generic REST and Generic Webhook providers.
request.path_help: Use path for Generic REST. Use URL only if you need to override the full endpoint.
request.json_help: Leave empty and use raw body below if the endpoint does not expect JSON.
request.latest_response_help: The request result is shown here immediately after execution. Full history is kept in Logs.

sync.title: Polling sync
sync.help: Use this when the provider does not push changes to Octodrop. The saved sync config defines how the next batch is fetched and where the checkpoint cursor is stored.
sync.no_schema: This provider does not define a sync schema.
sync.latest_result_help: This shows the last manual sync run from this page. Background runs and history are visible in Logs and Sync checkpoints.
sync.request_builder_help: Define the request used for each poll.
sync.path_help: Use path for Generic REST base URLs. Use full URL only when you need to override the whole endpoint.
sync.json_help: Leave empty unless the polling endpoint expects a JSON payload.

webhooks.title: Webhook definitions
webhooks.help: Inbound webhooks store raw events and process them asynchronously. Outbound webhooks can be called through automation actions or manual requests.
webhooks.signing_model_help: Signed outbound webhooks now include X-Octo-Timestamp and X-Octo-Signature. The signature is an HMAC SHA-256 over timestamp.payload. Inbound verification accepts that format and still supports the older body-only sha256= signature for compatibility.

mappings.title: Mapping profiles
mappings.help: Mappings stay declarative. Use them to turn inbound sync items into OCTO records without writing runtime code. Start with the guided fields, then only use raw JSON for advanced cases.
mappings.source_entity_help: This is the external/source shape label for humans. It does not need to exist as an OCTO entity.
mappings.target_entity_help: Use the OCTO entity id you want this mapping to create or update.
mappings.resource_key_help: Optional. Use this to target only sync items from one resource, such as contacts or invoices.
mappings.match_on_help: Comma-separated target fields used to find an existing OCTO record before updating it.
mappings.field_mappings_help: Each row maps one source value into one OCTO target field.
mappings.preview_help: Paste one sample source record here to see the mapped OCTO field values before you save the profile.
mappings.advanced_overrides_help: These fields are merged on top of the guided builder output. Use this only for advanced conditions or future mapping options.

logs.request_help: Outbound test calls and automation-driven provider calls are stored here for troubleshooting.
logs.webhook_events_help: Inbound webhook payloads are always stored first, then processed asynchronously by workers.
logs.sync_checkpoints_help: Polling integrations use checkpoints so later sync runs only process new or changed records.

guide.title: Setup guide
guide.help: Use this as the linear path for getting a connection ready. You usually only need the connection settings, the correct secrets, and a successful test run.
guide.review_description: Fill in the provider-specific fields below. Most integrations only need the basic connection settings plus the right auth mode.
guide.attach_secrets_progress: {{linked}} of {{total}} required secret slots are linked. Open the Secrets tab to attach or create them.
guide.last_tested: Last tested {{value}}. Use this after changing setup fields or secrets.
guide.test_description: Use Test connection once setup and secrets are ready. This confirms the runtime can actually authenticate.

setup.connection_setup_help: Keep this section focused on provider settings. Secrets are linked separately so credentials stay reusable and isolated from business logic.
setup.provider_settings_help: These are the main provider fields your team will usually configure first.
setup.group_help: These fields belong together and are part of this provider's setup schema.
setup.oauth_help: Use this when the provider authenticates through an OAuth2 browser flow. Generate the authorize URL, complete the provider login, then exchange the returned code so Octodrop can store and refresh the tokens safely.
setup.redirect_uri_help: Register this exact URL with the provider. If the provider redirects back with ?code=..., this page will prefill the code field automatically.
setup.authorization_code_help: After completing the provider login, paste the returned code parameter here if it was not auto-filled.
setup.advanced_config_help: These advanced provider fields are still driven by the provider schema, but are tucked away because most connections will not need them.
setup.full_config_help: This is the raw stored config. Use it for fields not yet modeled in the guided form.
setup.summary_help: This is the current runtime state of the connection, including provider, health, and audit timestamps.
setup.latest_test_result_help: Use Test connection after changing config or secrets. The result here is only the latest run; full request history is in Logs.

modal.create_secret_help: Secrets are encrypted, reusable, and resolved only at runtime by the worker.
modal.secret_name_help: Use a name your team will recognize when linking this secret to connections.
modal.secret_slot_help: The slot should match the named secret this provider expects, such as api_key, bearer_token, or signing_secret.
modal.secret_value_help: The value is stored encrypted and will not be shown again after save.

octo_ai.workspace_prepare_failed: The session was created, but its workspace could not be prepared.
octo_ai.delete_body: This will remove {{count}} change request(s) and related plans, revisions, and sandbox history. This cannot be undone.
octo_ai.create_description: Create a new workspace change request and open it directly in its sandbox. Sandbox state stays scoped to that request.
octo_ai.optional_context_placeholder: Short note to help the AI understand this requested change.

session_detail.history_only_description: This page is the session history only. Start a new sandbox session from the Octo AI list when you want to build again.

details.title: Request Details
details.description: Technical information is secondary to the main change-request flow.

summary.plan_approved_next: Plan approved. I prepared a validated revision. Next: Apply to Sandbox.

chat.empty: Describe the change you want to make. I will plan it first, then show the right workflow actions when there is something real to act on.

plan.default_summary: Describe the change, confirm the plan, test it in sandbox, then publish when ready.

placeholders.idle: Describe the change you want to make...
placeholders.planning: Describe what you want changed, or refine the plan...
placeholders.ready_to_apply: Review the validated revision, request changes, or add more instructions...
placeholders.sandbox_ready: Test the result, report issues, or request another revision...
placeholders.question_reply: Type the missing detail or correction here...
placeholders.question_clarification: Reply with clarification or extra instructions...

hints.confirm_plan: Approve the plan, validate the revision, and apply it to sandbox.
hints.save_detail: Save the missing detail directly from the inline fields.
hints.answer_in_chat: This request needs one more detail before it can be applied.
hints.view_scope: Review the current scope while you keep refining the request in chat.
hints.apply_revision: Apply the validated revision and refresh the preview automatically.
hints.applying: The preview will refresh automatically when the apply completes.
hints.publishing: Publishing this request to live now.
hints.view_live: Open the live workspace to confirm the published result.

preview.no_sandbox_workspace: No sandbox workspace is attached to this session yet.
preview.title: Sandbox workspace preview

provider_unavailable.title: OpenAI not connected
provider_unavailable.description: Connect an OpenAI key for this workspace to use Octo AI.

lists.no_changes: Describe the change in chat to generate a scoped plan.
lists.no_modules: Modules will appear here once the plan is scoped.

sandbox_not_ready.title: Sandbox not ready
sandbox_not_ready.active_description: Create the sandbox for this request, then keep working here with the live sandbox preview.
sandbox_not_ready.inactive_description: This session no longer has an active sandbox. Historical sessions stay readable, but only active sessions can launch a sandbox.

errors.openai_not_connected: OpenAI is not connected for this workspace.
errors.no_patchset_generated: No patchset was generated for this request.
errors.no_validated_revision: No validated revision was available after validation.
errors.patchset_not_validated: Patchset must be validated before apply: {{status}}
errors.prepare_latest_revision: Failed to prepare the latest revision
errors.no_validated_revision_apply: No validated revision was available to apply.
errors.apply_revision: Failed to apply the revision to sandbox
errors.no_release_available: No release was available to publish.
errors.restore_revision: Failed to restore the selected revision
errors.rollback_release: Failed to roll back the published release

header.review_plan: Review the plan below. When you approve it, Octo AI will apply the validated change to this sandbox automatically.
header.sandbox_updated: Sandbox updated. Keep chatting to refine it, or roll back the latest sandbox change.
header.needs_attention: The latest sandbox change needs attention. Ask Octo AI to revise it or review the details below.
header.describe_request: Describe what you want changed in this sandbox. Octo AI will explain the plan first, then apply it here after approval.

apply_summary.status_flow_body: Refresh {{moduleName}} and check the status bar plus the status action buttons on the form to see the new lifecycle state.
apply_summary.field_in_tab_body: Open {{moduleName}}, open a record, then check the {{tabLabel}} tab for the '{{fieldLabel}}' field.
apply_summary.field_added_body: Open {{moduleName}} and look for the new '{{fieldLabel}}' field on the affected form or view.
apply_summary.generic_body: Refresh {{moduleName}} in the sandbox to review the latest change.

hints.failed_validation: The last sandbox apply attempt failed validation. Ask Octo AI to revise the plan.
hints.reply_with_missing_detail: Reply below with the missing detail.

placeholders.confirm_plan: Tell Octo AI what you want changed about this plan...
placeholders.describe_change: Describe what you want changed in this sandbox...

empty.start_prompt: Start by telling Octo AI what to build or change in this sandbox.

errors.apply_latest_plan: Failed to apply the latest plan to this sandbox
errors.approved_plan_not_valid: The approved plan could not be validated for this sandbox.
errors.rollback: Failed to roll back the latest sandbox change

provider_secret_modal.openai_description: Add the workspace OpenAI API key to enable AI in Studio, Automations, Templates, and Octo AI.
provider_secret_modal.google_maps_description: Add a workspace server-side Google Maps / Places API key with billing and Places API enabled to use address autocomplete in forms.
provider_secret_modal.default_description: Add a workspace secret for this provider.
provider_secret_modal.permission_required: You do not have permission to manage workspace secrets. Ask a workspace admin to connect this provider in Settings.

automations.title: Automation
automations.import_description: Paste an automation JSON definition to create a new automation.
automations.delete_one_confirm: Delete this automation? This cannot be undone.
automations.delete_many_confirm: Delete {{count}} automation(s)? This cannot be undone.

automation_run_detail.title: Automation run
automation_run_detail.subtitle: Run details
automation_run_detail.trigger_payload_description: The payload and trigger data that started this automation run.
automation_run_detail.steps_description: Recorded step execution output for this run.

branding_description: Applies to the active workspace only.
workspace_admin_only: Only workspace admins can edit these settings.
regional_description: Set the default locale, timezone, and currency for this workspace.
currency_not_from_locale: Currency is separate from locale. Locale controls presentation only; currency controls the monetary unit.
enter_filter_value: Enter a value to filter by.

api_credentials.title: API Credentials
api_credentials.create_description: Create a scoped API key for a third-party system. The raw token is shown once after creation.
api_credentials.copy_description: This is the only time the full token is shown. Store it now.
api_credentials.name: Name
api_credentials.expires_help: Optional. Use this for vendor keys or short-lived rollout credentials.
api_credentials.revoke_before_delete_single: Revoke this API credential before deleting it.
api_credentials.delete_body: This will permanently remove {{count}} revoked API credential(s). Request logs will remain for audit history.
api_credentials.delete_single_body: This will permanently remove this revoked API credential. Request logs will remain for audit history.
api_credentials.active_must_revoke: Active API credentials must be revoked before they can be deleted.
api_credentials.details_description: Rotate and revoke from here. Scopes are fixed after creation.
api_credentials.allowed_scopes_description: Keep credentials narrow. Only grant what the destination system needs.
api_credentials.no_requests: No external API requests logged yet.

records_read.label: Records Read
records_read.help: Read records through the external API.

records_write.label: Records Write
records_write.help: Create and update records through the external API.

document_numbering.title: Document Numbering
document_numbering.load_failed: Failed to load document numbering settings
document_numbering.name: Name
document_numbering.delete_body_one: This permanently deletes the selected numbering sequence.
document_numbering.delete_body_many: This permanently deletes the selected numbering sequences.

detail.sequence_description: Use stable internal codes and business-friendly names. Changes only affect future records.
detail.assignment_warning: This sequence is already in use on {{count}} record(s). Changes here only apply to future assignments. Historical document numbers are not renumbered.
detail.name_help: Required. Use a clear business name like Quotes, Invoices, or Purchase Orders.
detail.code_help: Required. Use a stable internal key. This is for admins, not end users.
detail.target_entity_help: Required. Choose the record type that should receive this number.
detail.number_field_help: Required. This field will store the generated document number on the record.
detail.pattern_help: Required. Combine text and tokens like {YYYY} or {SEQ:4} to control the final format.
detail.field_token_help: Optional. Insert a token like {FIELD:invoice.state} to use a value from the target record in the document number.
detail.sort_order_help: Optional. Lower numbers sort earlier when admins view these sequences.
detail.scope_help: Choose whether one counter is shared globally, per entity value, or per workspace.
detail.reset_policy_help: Choose how often the sequence counter starts again.
detail.assign_on_help: Choose when the number should be assigned during the record lifecycle.
detail.scope_field_help: Required for entity scope. Records with different values in this field get separate counters.
detail.description_help: Optional. Add a short admin note about when this sequence should be used.
detail.notes_help: Optional. Use this for implementation notes, migration details, or edge-case handling.
detail.trigger_statuses_help: Choose the workflow statuses that should assign the number.
detail.live_preview_description: Preview uses the current counter bucket and your current workspace context.
detail.preview_hint: Complete the core fields to see a preview.
detail.token_help_description: Keep patterns simple and stable. These tokens are supported in v1.

secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.name: Name
secrets.provider_key_help: Optional. Useful once one workspace stores secrets for several providers.
secrets.secret_key_help: Optional slot name such as `api_token`, `client_secret`, or `signing_secret`.
secrets.secret_value_help: Secret values are encrypted and not shown again after save.
secrets.delete_body: This will remove {{count}} secret(s). Connections using these secrets will need to be relinked.

access_policies.title: Access Policies
access_policies.create_description: Create the profile here, then add its access rules on the next page.
access_policies.name: Name
access_policies.name_help: Required. Use a clear name like Sales, Finance, or Customer Admin.
access_policies.key_help: Optional. Use a stable internal key if you want one, otherwise you can leave this blank.
access_policies.description_help: Optional. Add a short note explaining who this profile is for or what it grants.
access_policies.delete_body: This will permanently remove {{count}} access profile(s). This cannot be undone.

detail.condition_json_invalid: Condition JSON must be valid JSON.
detail.profile_description: Names are user-facing. Keys are stable references you can reuse later.
detail.add_rule_description: Use exact ids such as a module id, entity id, field id, or module_id:action_id.
detail.resource_type_help: Choose what kind of thing this rule controls.
detail.resource_id_help: Required. Use the exact id for the module, entity, field, or action you want this rule to apply to.
detail.access_help: Choose the access level this rule should enforce.
detail.priority_help: Lower numbers run first. Default is 100.
detail.condition_json_help: Optional. Leave blank to apply this rule everywhere the resource matches.
detail.rules_description: Rules are evaluated as a restrictive overlay on top of the base workspace role.
detail.no_rules: No rules on this profile yet.

webhook_subscriptions.title: Webhook Subscriptions
webhook_subscriptions.create_description: Subscribe an external endpoint to Octodrop events. Matching events are delivered asynchronously by workers.
webhook_subscriptions.headers_json_invalid: Headers JSON must be valid JSON
webhook_subscriptions.name: Name
webhook_subscriptions.delete_body: This will permanently remove {{count}} webhook subscription(s). This cannot be undone.

detail.title: Subscription Details
detail.description: Control target, pattern, status, signing, and header overrides here.
detail.delivery_description: Recent delivery outcome and signing state for this endpoint.

security_center.title: Security Center
security_center.subtitle: Superadmin-only monitoring for API denials, webhook failures, integration failures, module audit, and platform access.
security_center.feed_description: Use this feed for investigation. Critical production alerts still need external Slack/email/on-call routing.
security_center.operational_note_body: This page is for superadmin review and incident triage. It does not replace production alert routing to Slack/email/on-call, and it intentionally uses a gated internal DB read path to show global security telemetry across workspaces.

email_connections.title: Email Connections
email_connections.delete_body: This will permanently remove {{count}} email connection(s). This cannot be undone.
email_connections.connection_name_help: Required. Use a clear name like Support SMTP or Marketing Outbound.
email_connections.status_help: Optional. Disable the connection without deleting it.
email_connections.smtp_host_help: Required. Enter the outgoing mail server host provided by your email provider.
email_connections.port_help: Usually `587` for STARTTLS or `465` for SSL/TLS.
email_connections.security_help: Choose the transport security required by the server.
email_connections.password_source_help: Choose whether the SMTP password is stored directly here or pulled from Secrets.
email_connections.username_help: Usually the mailbox email address or SMTP username.
email_connections.saved_secret_help: Optional. Pick a stored secret instead of entering the password directly.
email_connections.secret_id_override_help: Optional. Use this only if you need to point at a secret id directly.
email_connections.password_help: Optional if your server does not require authentication. Otherwise use the SMTP password or app password.
email_connections.from_email_help: Optional. Leave blank to let the provider default the sender address.
email_connections.from_name_help: Optional display name that appears in the recipient inbox.

email_templates.title: Email Templates
email_templates.delete_body_one: This permanently deletes the selected email template.
email_templates.delete_body_many: This permanently deletes the selected email templates.
email_templates.restart_api_hint: If this endpoint was just added, restart the API server.

documents_templates_page.delete_body_one: This will permanently remove the selected document template.
documents_templates_page.delete_body_many: This will permanently remove the selected document templates.
documents_templates_page.restart_api_hint: If this endpoint was just added, restart the API server.

template_studio.document_template_details_description: Set the name, description, entity, and filename pattern for this document template.
template_studio.email_template_details_description: Set the name, description, entity, and default sending connection for this template.
template_studio.template_entity_hint: Templates render fields from one entity.
template_studio.filename_pattern_hint: Tip: use record fields to make filenames unique.
template_studio.document_content_description: Write the main HTML plus any optional header and footer blocks.
template_studio.page_setup_description: Choose the paper size and page margins for the generated PDF.
template_studio.message_content_description: Write the subject and HTML body for the email template.
template_studio.email_agent_message: Describe the email template change you want and I will draft an update.
template_studio.document_agent_message: Describe the document template change you want and I will draft an update.
template_studio.default_agent_message: Describe the template change you want and I will draft an update.
template_studio.choose_entity_preview_email: Choose an entity above to render this email with placeholders, or choose a record to preview real data.
template_studio.select_entity_template_tab_preview: Select an entity in the Template tab to see a placeholder preview.
template_studio.save_template_to_see_history: Save the template to see history.
template_studio.save_template_to_see_jobs: Save the template to see jobs.
template_studio.validation_idle: Validation runs automatically while you edit.
template_studio.ai_assistant_description: Use the assistant to draft and refine changes in this editor.
template_studio.validation_description: Review validation results and warnings while you work.
template_studio.openai_not_connected_description: Connect an OpenAI key for this workspace to use template AI.

automation_editor.delete_confirmation: Delete this automation? This cannot be undone.
automation_editor.default_agent_message: Describe the automation change you want and I will draft an update.
automation_editor.openai_not_connected_description: Connect an OpenAI key for this workspace to use Automation AI.
automation_editor.validation_idle: Validation runs automatically while you edit.
automation_editor.schedule_interval_required: Schedule interval must be greater than 0 minutes.
automation_editor.at_least_one_step_required: At least one step is required.
automation_editor.select_step_to_edit: Select a step from the flow to edit it.
automation_editor.notify_help: Send an in-app notification to one or more workspace users.
automation_editor.condition_help: Only continue if this rule matches the trigger or prior-step data.
automation_editor.delay_until_help: Pause this automation until a specific date and time.
automation_editor.delay_for_help: Pause this automation for a relative amount of time.
automation_editor.foreach_help: Run the selected action once for each item in a list, such as query results or selected records.
automation_editor.send_email_help: Send an email using direct addresses, record fields, related records, or a template.
automation_editor.generate_document_help: Generate a document from a template and attach it to a record.
automation_editor.create_record_help: Create a new record in any entity using dynamic values from the trigger or earlier steps.
automation_editor.update_record_help: Update an existing record by applying a patch of field changes.
automation_editor.query_records_help: Find records from an entity so later steps can branch, loop, or update them.
automation_editor.add_chatter_help: Post an activity note or comment onto a record.
automation_editor.integration_request_help: Call a configured integration connection using its provider settings and authentication.
automation_editor.integration_sync_help: Run a polling sync against a configured integration connection and optionally emit item events.
automation_editor.module_action_help: Run a module action against a target record or selection.
automation_editor.configure_step_help: Configure what this step should do and what later steps can reuse.
automation_editor.drop_step_to_start: Drop a step here to start {{name}}
automation_editor.drop_here_first: Drop here to place it first in {{name}}
automation_editor.drop_between_items: Drop here to place this step between items
automation_editor.condition_branch_help: This step can branch into `Then` and `Else` steps.
automation_editor.drop_step_when_true: Drop a step here to run it when true.
automation_editor.steps_run_when_true: These steps run when the condition is true.
automation_editor.drop_step_when_false: Drop a step here to run it when false.
automation_editor.steps_run_when_false: These steps run when the condition is false.
automation_editor.drop_step_repeat_each_item: Drop a step here to repeat it for each item.
automation_editor.flow_heading: Build what happens after the trigger
automation_editor.flow_description: Click the trigger or a step to edit it in a focused drawer. Add steps where they belong. `Then / Else` branches only appear on Condition steps.
automation_editor.no_steps_start_flow: No steps yet. Add a step to start building the flow.
automation_editor.step_setup_help: Start by choosing what kind of step this is and, for actions, what it should do.
automation_editor.repeat_settings_help: Choose the list to loop over and how each repeated item should be referenced.
automation_editor.repeat_over_help: Use a trigger list, query results, or stored variable.
automation_editor.recipients_help: Choose which workspace users should receive this notification.
automation_editor.add_workspace_users_help: Add one or more workspace users to notify.
automation_editor.notification_content_help: Write the message and choose the visual severity.
automation_editor.title: Title
automation_editor.notification_title_help: What the user sees as the notification heading.
automation_editor.notification_body_help: Main message text. You can still use refs like {{trigger.record_id}}.
automation_editor.severity_help: Optional. This changes the visual style only. Default is Info.
automation_editor.link_help: Choose what should open when someone clicks the notification.
automation_editor.trigger_record_link_help: Optional. Opens the record from the trigger event.
automation_editor.specific_record_link_help: Optional. Opens a specific record. The record ID starts with {{trigger.record_id}} but you can change it.
automation_editor.custom_url_link_help: Optional. Opens an internal route or external website.
automation_editor.no_link_help: Optional. Choose what should open when someone clicks the notification.
automation_editor.record_entity_help: Choose the entity that should open from the notification.
automation_editor.record_id_help: Defaults to {{trigger.record_id}}. Change it if this notification should open a different record.
automation_editor.custom_url_help: Use an internal route like /home or an external URL like https://example.com.
automation_editor.email_recipients_help: Choose who should receive this email, whether that comes from direct addresses, record fields, related records, or expressions.
automation_editor.direct_email_addresses_help: Optional comma-separated addresses. Leave blank if you only want to use recipients from fields or related records.
automation_editor.target_email_field_help: Only needed if the related record has more than one email field.
automation_editor.recipient_expression_help: Optional. Add extra addresses, refs, or expressions in addition to the selected recipient sources.
automation_editor.email_content_help: Set how this email is sent, where merge fields should read from, and what the message should contain.
automation_editor.default_connection_help: Optional. Leave blank to use the workspace default.
automation_editor.email_template_help: Optional. Leave blank to write the subject and body here instead.
automation_editor.entity_for_merge_fields_help: Optional. Pick the entity whose fields should be available in this email.
automation_editor.record_for_merge_fields_help: Optional. Use a record ID, a ref like {{trigger.record_id}}, or leave blank to follow the trigger context.
automation_editor.subject_override_help: Optional. Leave blank to use the template subject.
automation_editor.subject_required_without_template: Required when no template is selected.
automation_editor.email_body_help: Required when no template is selected. Write the plain-text body here.
automation_editor.attachments_help: Choose whether this email should include generated files or attachments from a record.
automation_editor.include_attachments_help: Turn this on only if the email should include generated files or attachments from a record.
automation_editor.attachment_purpose_help: Optional. Attach linked files with this purpose from the selected record.
automation_editor.attachment_entity_help: Optional. Leave blank to reuse the email merge entity above.
automation_editor.attachment_record_help: Optional. Use a record ID or a ref if the files live on a different record.
automation_editor.attachment_field_help: Optional extra source if files already live in an attachments field on the record.
automation_editor.document_setup_help: Choose the template, target record, and optional attachment purpose for the generated file.
automation_editor.document_purpose_help: Optional attachment purpose tag, like `handover_pdf` or `quote_pdf`.
automation_editor.filename_conflict_mode_help: Choose what happens when generated documents use the same filename.
automation_editor.record_or_trigger_help: Paste record ID or use trigger.
automation_editor.request_setup_help: Choose the connection and the endpoint this request should call.
automation_editor.path_help: Use path for the provider base URL. Leave blank if you want to call a full URL directly.
automation_editor.request_headers_help: Add request headers one by one. Use Insert on the refs above when a header value should come from the trigger or an earlier step.
automation_editor.query_parameters_help: Add URL query parameters one by one. Leave this empty if the request does not need query values.
automation_editor.request_body_help: Use this if the request needs a JSON body, a raw body, or you want to edit the raw headers/query objects directly.
automation_editor.json_body_help: Use this for structured payloads. Leave empty and use raw body only if the endpoint does not expect JSON.
automation_editor.sync_setup_help: Choose the connection, checkpoint scope, and whether this sync should emit events or run in the background.
automation_editor.checkpoint_scope_help: Use different scopes when one connection syncs more than one resource.
automation_editor.emit_item_events_help: Turn this on if later automations should react to each returned item.
automation_editor.queue_in_background_help: Use this when the sync might take a while and you do not need the result immediately.
automation_editor.sync_request_options_help: Override the saved sync request or cursor settings for this automation run.
automation_editor.sync_headers_help: Override or add sync request headers one by one.
automation_editor.sync_query_parameters_help: Override or add sync query parameters one by one.
automation_editor.action_target_help: Choose which record or records this module action should run against.
automation_editor.record_setup_help: Choose which entity to create and where to store the result for later steps.
automation_editor.field_values_help: Fill out the record one field at a time. Use Insert on the refs above when a value should come from the trigger or an earlier step.
automation_editor.pick_field_for_type_aware_input: Pick a field first to get a type-aware value input.
automation_editor.raw_values_json_help: Use this only if you need nested objects or a shape that is easier to paste as raw JSON.
automation_editor.record_target_help: Choose the entity and record to update, and optionally store the returned record.
automation_editor.field_changes_help: Only add the fields you want to change. Anything you leave out is untouched.
automation_editor.raw_patch_json_help: Use this only if you need nested objects or want to paste a prepared patch object.
automation_editor.query_setup_help: Choose the entity, optional search text, and how many records should be returned.
automation_editor.search_text_help: Optional text search. Leave blank if you only want to use filter rules.
automation_editor.limit_help: Only the first matching records are returned to later steps.
automation_editor.quick_filters_help: Add simple rules to narrow down records. These compile into the same advanced filter JSON behind the scenes.
automation_editor.no_value_needed_for_operator: No value needed for this operator
automation_editor.use_record_field_for_type_input: Use record.field_name format to get a type-aware value input.
automation_editor.search_and_raw_filter_options_help: Use this for search field limits or a more complex filter expression than the quick rules above can represent.
automation_editor.search_fields_help: Optional comma-separated field IDs to limit text search.
automation_editor.entry_setup_help: Choose the record, entry type, and message that should be added as chatter.
automation_editor.delay_settings_help: Choose whether the step should wait for a relative amount of time or until a specific datetime.
automation_editor.condition_rule_help: Choose the field to check, how to compare it, and what value should be matched.
automation_editor.stop_automation_if_condition_false: Stop automation if condition is false
automation_editor.use_last_step_id_output: Use the last step's ID output
automation_editor.use_records_from_query_step: Use records from a query step
automation_editor.use_records_from_stored_variable: Use records from a stored variable
automation_editor.trigger_setup_help: Choose what starts this automation and fill in the matching trigger details.
automation_editor.run_feedback_help: Choose whether users see background progress and toast messages when this automation runs.
automation_editor.schedule_interval_help: Use a simple interval first. The shared scheduler will enqueue this automation in the background.
automation_editor.webhook_setup_help: Run this automation when an inbound integration webhook is received. Narrow it only if you need to.
automation_editor.webhook_connection_help: Optional. Leave empty to react to any configured integration connection.
automation_editor.event_key_help: Optional. Only set this if the flow should run for one webhook event type.
automation_editor.webhook_data_available: Webhook data available to the flow
automation_editor.webhook_data_available_help: Use these payload and metadata paths in later steps.
automation_editor.queue_sample_inbound_webhook: Queue a sample inbound webhook against the saved automation.
automation_editor.trigger_rules_help: Optional. Use these only if the automation should run only in specific cases.
automation_editor.webhook_runs_when_match: This automation will run whenever the selected webhook trigger matches.
automation_editor.event_runs_every_time: This automation will run every time the selected trigger event happens.
automation_editor.pick_suggested_field_or_custom_path: Pick a suggested field or type a custom path.
automation_editor.advanced_logic_help: Use this only if the simple rules above are not enough.
automation_editor.advanced_trigger_condition_json_help: Optional full condition DSL over `trigger.*` values. Leave empty unless you need advanced branching logic.
automation_editor.automation_json_help: Edit the full automation definition here, then apply it back into the flow.
automation_editor.trigger_drawer_description: Choose what starts this automation and add any optional trigger rules.
automation_editor.test_webhook_trigger_help: Queue a test run using a sample inbound webhook event against the saved automation.
automation_editor.payload_json_help: This becomes `trigger.payload` in the automation context.
automation_editor.json_must_be_object: Automation JSON must be an object.
automation_editor.rendered_with_jinja_context: Rendered with Jinja context: record, trigger, branding.
automation_editor.trigger_summary_schedule_help: This automation runs from the shared scheduler.
automation_editor.trigger_summary_event_help: Keep this simple where possible. Most automations only need a trigger and a flow.
automation_editor.webhook_rules_help: Use extra rules only if connection and event key are not enough. These rules run against webhook payload data.
automation_editor.event_rules_help: Most automations can leave this empty and run whenever the trigger happens.

email_outbox.title: Email Outbox
email_outbox.delete_body_one: This will permanently remove the selected outbox item.
email_outbox.delete_body_many: This will permanently remove the selected outbox items.
email_outbox.restart_api_hint: If this endpoint was just added, restart the API server.

external_api_docs.title: Octodrop External API
external_api_docs.subtitle: Client-ready documentation for records, files, automations, and signed webhooks. The docs are public; the API itself requires a scoped
external_api_docs.reference_description: Use these links to read the full contract, test requests, or generate clients.
external_api_docs.quickstart_description: Create a scoped API credential in Settings, then call metadata first to discover installed entity IDs.

swagger.label: Swagger UI
swagger.description: Interactive reference for testing calls with an API key.

redoc.label: ReDoc
redoc.description: Clean reference view for reading the full public API.

openapi.label: OpenAPI JSON
openapi.description: Machine-readable schema for SDKs and generated clients.

guide.label: Guide
guide.description: Production integration notes, auth, retries, and webhooks.

events.label: Events
events.description: Webhook event names and payload expectations.

contract.auth_value: Send X-Api-Key on every /ext/v1 request. Do not put keys in query strings.
contract.scopes_value: Use one credential per integration with only the scopes it needs.
contract.rate_limits_value: Default is 300 requests per 60 seconds per API credential. Handle 429 with Retry-After.
contract.pagination_value: Use limit and cursor. The current hard cap is 200 records per page.
contract.webhooks_value: Verify X-Octo-Timestamp and X-Octo-Signature before processing events.
contract.errors_value: Expect stable JSON errors with ok=false and errors[].code/message/path.

handoff_items.credential: Create one credential per external system and grant minimum scopes.
handoff_items.keys: Store keys server-side only and rotate them on a regular schedule.
handoff_items.metadata: Use metadata routes to discover entity and field IDs instead of hardcoding labels.
handoff_items.errors: Handle 401, 403, 429, and cursor pagination in the integration client.

users.admin_required: At least one admin user is required in every workspace.
users.admin_required_title: At least one admin is required in every workspace.
users.user_removed_with_auth: User removed and auth account deleted.
users.platform_invite_sent: Platform invite sent to {{email}} ({{flow}} flow). No workspace assigned until first login.
users.admin_required_manage: Admin role is required to invite and manage users.
users.platform_invite_description: Invite a user without assigning them to the current workspace (workspace created on first login).
users.workspace_users_description: Everyone who can access this workspace.
users.no_users: No users in this workspace yet.
users.delete_auth_user: Also delete this user from Supabase Auth (global account delete)
users.superadmin_required: Superadmin required for global auth deletion.
users.change_email_description: Update login email for this user.
users.no_access_profiles: No access profiles yet. Create them in Settings -> Access Policies.

settings.title: Profile
settings.description: Password, theme, preferences, and profile details.

users.title: Users & Roles
users.description: Manage users, roles, and access policies.

access_policies.title: Access Policies
access_policies.description: Profile-based app, entity, field, and action access rules.

security.title: Security Center
security.description: Superadmin-only monitoring for API denials, webhook failures, integration failures, module audit, and platform access.

documents_templates.title: Document Templates
documents_templates.description: HTML to PDF templates with placeholders.

document_numbering.title: Document Numbering
document_numbering.description: Shared numbering rules for quotes, orders, invoices, and other business records.

automations.title: Automations
automations.description: Workflow automations, triggers, runs, and publishing.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Connection setup, request logs, syncs, mappings, and webhook endpoints.

studio.title: Studio
studio.description: Build, edit, validate, and install system and business modules.

api_credentials.title: API Credentials
api_credentials.description: Scoped API keys for external systems connecting to Octodrop.

webhook_subscriptions.title: Webhook Subscriptions
webhook_subscriptions.description: Send signed Octodrop events to external endpoints.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: System health checks and perf diagnostics.

apps_page.title: Apps
apps_page.delete_body: Choose whether to keep records created by this module, or delete everything.
apps_page.remove_keep_records_help: The app is archived or disabled, but your workspace data remains.
apps_page.delete_module_records_help: Irreversible. Removes all records for entities defined by this module, and any other module using the same entities.
apps_page.also_type_delete_to_confirm: Also type DELETE to confirm record deletion.
apps_page.login_required: Please log in to view apps.
apps_page.display_order_integer: Display order must be an integer
apps_page.marketplace_delete_prompt: Type DELETE to permanently remove this marketplace app.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: Theme, developer tools, and account details.
preferences.developer_mode_help: Shows entity, module, and view IDs, hashes, and debug links.

templates.title: Templates
templates.description: HTML to PDF templates with placeholders.

documents_history.title: Generated Documents
documents_history.description: History of generated PDFs linked to records.

connections.title: Connections
connections.description: Connect a provider to send email.

templates.title: Templates
templates.description: Reusable email templates for automations and actions.

email_diagnostics.title: Email Diagnostics
email_diagnostics.description: Connection status, recent failures, and worker health.

preview.invalid_draft_json: Preview unavailable: draft JSON is invalid.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: Ask for changes and get PatchSets.
agent.intro: Describe the change you want and I will draft an update.
agent.describe_change_for_module: Describe the change you want for {{moduleName}} and I will draft an update.
agent.openai_not_connected_description: Connect an OpenAI key for this workspace to use Studio AI.

delete_modal.draft_body: This will remove the draft without affecting any installed module.
delete_modal.remove_keep_records_help: The app is archived or disabled, but your workspace data remains.
delete_modal.delete_module_records_help: Irreversible. Removes all records for entities defined by this module, and any other module using the same entities.
delete_modal.also_type_delete_to_confirm: Also type DELETE to confirm record deletion.

delete_blocked.title: Module has records
delete_blocked.body: This module has {{count}} record(s). You can archive it, or force delete to remove all records.
